source_issue.title: Add GHA to ensure dependency mappings are up-to-date

summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue suggests adding a GitHub Action (GHA) to check that the dependency mappings for `Zope`, `Products.CMFCore`, and `plone.base` are current. The goal is to compare these dependencies against what is recorded in the `config/default/pyproject.toml.j2` file. If discrepancies are found, the GHA will trigger a warning.

## What needs to be done
You'll need to create a GitHub Action that:
1. Fetches the current dependencies for the specified projects.
2. Compares these to the existing entries in `config/default/pyproject.toml.j2`.
3. Issues a warning if there are any differences.

## Helpful background
A GitHub Action is a tool to automate workflows in your GitHub repository. This enhancement will help keep our dependencies up-to-date, which is vital for maintaining the stability and security of the project.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with creating GitHub Actions. The [official documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/actions) is a great starting point.
2. Explore how to fetch dependencies from `Zope`, `Products.CMFCore`, and `plone.base`.
3. Look at the `config/default/pyproject.toml.j2` to understand the format of the data you'll be comparing against.

Feel free to ask questions if you need clarification!
